mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals expanding labor income and consumer confidence, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Over a 3-year rolling window, the correlation between PAYEMS monthly change and 20-day forward SPX returns is not positive (rank IC < 0.03) or the mean 20-day return following positive PAYEMS changes is not statistically greater than zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the unemployment rate above 5.0% for two consecutive months signals labor market deterioration, leading to reduced consumer spending and earnings downgrades, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: After trigger activation, 60-day SPX forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances over a 10-year backtest, or the average return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When both the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) and CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) are above their 12-month moving averages, monetary policy is actively tightening to combat inflation, compressing equity valuations via higher discount rates and margin pressure, leading to negative forward returns.
failure_condition: The negative signal fails to materialize in at least 60% of instances when both series are above their moving averages, or the average 20-day forward return in those instances is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During the early phase of a post-recession recovery (GDPC1 trough-to-peak expansion with less than 12 months of positive growth), rising housing starts (HOUST) signal strengthening residential investment and consumer confidence, amplifying positive forward equity returns.
regime_hint: post-recession recovery phase (GDPC1 rising from trough, less than 12 months)
failure_condition: In the defined regime, the average 60-day SPX return when HOUST increases month-over-month is not positive or is not greater than the regime's overall average return by a statistically significant margin (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) exceeding 1% signals an abrupt contraction in manufacturing output and supply chain stress, which immediately depresses earnings expectations and triggers short-term equity selloffs.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO drops by more than 1% month-over-month
failure_condition: After a qualifying INDPRO drop, the average 5-day forward SPX return is not negative, or is greater than -0.5% (p > 0.10), or more than 40% of instances produce positive returns.

mechanism_narrative: When both retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) are rising concurrently month-over-month, it signals broad-based consumer demand strength, leading to upward revisions in GDP forecasts and positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal does not produce a positive average 20-day return over a 5-year walking window, or the hit rate (positive return) is below 55% in rolling evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is above 5.0%, monetary policy is restrictive enough to eventually slow economic activity and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a quarter horizon as the full transmission lags take effect.
failure_condition: After trigger, the average 60-day SPX return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or the rank IC over the full sample is below -0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real retail sales (RSAFS) month-over-month signals current consumer spending strength that directly improves near-term corporate earnings and investor sentiment, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The mean 5-day forward SPX return following a month-over-month increase in RSAFS is not positive (p > 0.05) or the rank IC over any 2-year rolling window is below 0.03.
